decisions.candidate_summary: Add a standing analytical lens for AI tooling vendor due diligence: when evaluating AI development tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot), proactively surface compliance posture (BAA availability, FedRAMP status, data routing architecture, audit trail capabilities) alongside capability assessment. Flag cloud-only architectures without BAA as presumptively non-compliant for SEC-registered RIA context.

reasoning.signal_assessment: Signal strength 0.86 is high and genuine. The candidate proposes a specific, actionable analytical lens with enumerated compliance checkpoints rather than a vague preference. Synthesized from 18 source posts including Cursor compliance discussions and Claude Code evaluation context. First appearance (prior_appearances: 0) - not stuck or noise-amplified. The specificity of the compliance checklist (BAA, FedRAMP, data routing, audit trails) indicates real signal extraction, not hallucinated pattern.
reasoning.memory_consistency: Strongly consistent with existing memory. Directly extends fb3193bf (single-vendor AI dependency as institutional risk) and 477a0a46 (Anthropic platform reliability as vendor risk factor). No contradiction with any existing entry. Does not duplicate - no current memory establishes this specific BAA/FedRAMP/audit-trail checklist as a standing due diligence lens. Target section (Infrastructure Architecture / Vendor Due Diligence) is appropriate for a profile-level analytical addition.
reasoning.eval_alignment: Eval history is empty - this is the first session with eval scoring available. Cannot draw inference from past similar changes. However, change_log shows related vendor-risk-oriented changes (c2-mem-001, c4-mem-001) were applied in prior cycles with no recorded negative outcomes. The pattern of applying compliance-oriented analytical additions has not caused known harm, though positive outcomes are also not yet measured.
reasoning.risk_assessment: Risk is bounded and reversible. Shadow mode application means no live configuration impact - this becomes a test case for the next eval cycle. Profile diffs are additive and removable. No dependencies on other candidates. SOURCE_NEW flag applies (source_reliability_score 0.5 default) but this is expected for a fresh synthesis path and does not indicate unreliability. Candidate is specific enough to generate a testable hypothesis.

hypothesis.prediction: When the analytical lens is active, AI tooling evaluations in the user's workflow will include compliance posture assessment (BAA/FedRAMP/audit trail) as a leading finding rather than a secondary caveat, reducing the likelihood of compliance-blind recommendations entering formal review.
hypothesis.expected_magnitude: +0.15 on compliance-relevant content in AI tooling recommendations
hypothesis.falsification_condition: If AI tooling evaluations over the next 14 days do not surface compliance posture as a leading finding (BAA, FedRAMP, audit trail presence/absence explicitly stated before capability assessment), or if the user manually adds compliance notes that the lens should have surfaced, the hypothesis is falsified.

decision_record.session_notes: Single-candidate batch. The candidate is well-formed with clear target section, detailed rationale, and source attribution. The digest pipeline produced one candidate from 18 source posts, suggesting appropriate filtering. The candidate extends the user's established vendor risk monitoring pattern (documented in c2-mem-001 and c4-mem-001) into a specific compliance-first evaluation methodology for AI tooling - a timely addition given the referenced Claude Code evaluation context. No eval baseline exists yet; this decision will help establish one. The _meta schema note about change_log summary fields is acknowledged but did not affect this evaluation.
